body_txt: What do you get when a child believes they know better than adults? This time, they can prove it. Almost always on the side of good, most likely belonging to the Chaotic Good category, this character isn't an indiscriminate back-answerer like the Bratty Half-Pint but rather steps up when an unreasonable or outright abusive authority figure tries to do something stupid or horrible (or just simply when adults do something he or she finds stupid), instead of submitting quietly to their authority. What separates them from your average Kid Hero is their snarkiness about it, at least at first. Unlike the Bratty Half-Pint , who is almost always proudly and shamelessly antagonistic and mostly exists to make The Hero 's life miserable, the Mouthy Kid is almost always the protagonist and / or close to The Hero (while being a Downplayed Trope ). In American sitcoms, this character type became increasingly popular from The '80s onward, with iconic characters like Arnold on Diff'rent Strokes setting the standard. This was seen as a liberating change from the 1950s portrayals of naive, sweet children modeling "good citizenship" in Leave It to Beaver , My Three Sons , Family Affair and other shows. In contrast, the Mouthy Kid generally signals a rebellious and countercultural attitude from the writers. As a result, this character type is uncommon in authoritarian cultures, in which the Mouthy Kid is more likely to be slapped upside the head and shipped off to Military School , if not given Ritalin, Risperdal, or something else to smooth out their phasic dopamine activity. This is a natural evolution of a child Wise Beyond Their Years into a more sardonic and sassy direction. Not to be confused with From the Mouths of Babes , which is a different kind of Mouthy Kid, though they can overlap if the kid uses swear words between their snarking. See also: Plucky Girl , Little Miss Snarker , Affection-Hating Kid , and Wise Beyond Their Years . Contrast Constantly Curious , who talks as much but has less sass.
